column1: # mapping_set_description: "SSSOM mappings from DataCite Metadata Schema 4.6 properties and controlled vocabulary terms to Schema.org. Schema.org targets only; DataCite-to-DCTERMS/DCAT-AP mappings are maintained in a separate artefact."
#   - Subject IRIs for sub-elements use nested paths (e.g. property/creator/creatorName).
#     These resolve via the redirect workflow to the canonical flat property files.
#   - dateType values with no schema.org equivalent (Accepted, Submitted, Withdrawn, Other, Valid)
#     are intentionally omitted; they are covered in the DataCite-DCTERMS mapping.
#     Note: schema:validFrom is NOT in the domain of CreativeWork/Dataset and was therefore removed.
#   - object_id values use https://schema.org/ (HTTPS) per current schema.org convention.

column7: comment
DataCite's mandatory DOI maps directly to schema:identifier as a PropertyValue
The scheme name (e.g. DOI) is the propertyID of a schema:PropertyValue
Primary research agents map to schema:creator
schema:author is a subproperty of schema:creator; DataCite creators are broader
Full display name of a creator
nameType=Personal drives rdf:type schema:Person on the agent node
nameType=Organizational drives rdf:type schema:Organization
Direct lexical match; both refer to a person's given name
Direct lexical match; both refer to a person's family name
Persistent identifier (e.g. ORCID) for the creator; modelled as a schema:PropertyValue
Scheme name (e.g. ORCID) is the propertyID of the identifier PropertyValue
Resolvable URL of the identifier scheme (e.g. https://orcid.org)
Organization with which the creator is affiliated
Persistent identifier for the affiliation (e.g. ROR); modelled as a PropertyValue
Scheme name (e.g. ROR) is the propertyID of the affiliation identifier
Resolvable URL of the affiliation identifier scheme
Primary title of the resource maps to schema:name
titleType qualifies which kind of title it is; individual values drive serialisation choices (see vocab rows below)
An alternative title is serialised as schema:alternateName
A subtitle is a secondary title; schema:alternateName is the appropriate slot
A translated title is associated with the concept of a translated work; relatedMatch because workTranslation is a CreativeWork not a string literal
Variant titles not covered by specific types; closeMatch because schema:alternateName is slightly narrower
Entity making the resource available
Persistent identifier for the publisher; modelled as a PropertyValue
Scheme name (e.g. ROR) is the propertyID of the publisher identifier
Resolvable URL of the publisher identifier scheme
Year the resource was or will be made publicly available
DataCite Subject covers both formal topic linkage and free-text tagging; broader than schema:about alone
Free-text subject terms relate informally to schema:keywords; relatedMatch because DataCite Subject also carries URIs and codes
When subject carries a valueURI and subjectScheme it can be serialised as a schema:DefinedTerm within a DefinedTermSet
Name of the controlled vocabulary (e.g. LCSH); maps to the name of the DefinedTermSet
Resolvable URL of the controlled vocabulary
URI identifying the subject term itself; modelled as the identifier of a DefinedTerm
Classification code within the scheme; schema:termCode exists for exactly this purpose
Non-primary contributing agent
The contributor's role (e.g. ProjectManager) is expressed as roleName on a schema:Role wrapper
Full display name of the contributor
Given name of the contributor
Family name of the contributor
Persistent identifier for the contributor; modelled as a PropertyValue
Scheme name (e.g. ORCID) is the propertyID of the identifier
Resolvable URL of the name identifier scheme
Organizational affiliation of the contributor
Persistent identifier for the contributor's affiliation
Scheme name (e.g. ROR) for the affiliation identifier
Resolvable URL of the affiliation identifier scheme
Generic date container; broader than any single schema.org date property
dateType qualifies the date value via PropertyValue.propertyID
Availability date approximates publication date; closeMatch because Available may precede formal journal publication
Date copyright was asserted maps directly to schema:copyrightYear
Data collection is a form of creation event; closeMatch because collection period != file creation date
Temporal span the resource describes or covers
Date the resource was created
Issuance to a repository approximates publication; closeMatch because Issued can precede public availability
Date of last modification
Free-text annotation about the date value (e.g. historical period)
Language of the resource content
Free-text resource type description; closeMatch because schema:description is broader
General type drives the JSON-LD @type; closeMatch because additionalType is typically a URI not an enum value
Series of visual representations possibly including sound
DataCite Award is broader than schema:Grant (covers non-monetary awards); Grant is a narrower schema.org class
Written work bound as a book
A chapter within a book
Aggregation of resources
Executable notebook (e.g. Jupyter); closeMatch because SoftwareSourceCode covers source but not the literate-programming execution model
Academic paper submitted to a conference
Volume of academic papers from a conference
Scholarly paper describing a dataset
Structured set of data
Academic dissertation or thesis
A non-persistent time-based occurrence
Visual representation other than text
Resource requiring user interaction; closeMatch because InteractiveResource covers training modules and portals beyond runnable software
Physical device used to collect or measure data; schema.org has no dedicated Instrument class so Product is used
Scholarly publication issued in regular installments
Peer-reviewed article in a journal
closeMatch: DataCite Model includes conceptual/mathematical models; schema:3DModel is too narrow; no better schema.org class exists
Formal document describing how research outputs are managed; schema:Report is the closest class
Evaluation of scholarly work by peers in the same field
Physical artifact or specimen; schema:Product is the closest tangible-item class without dcterms:PhysicalObject
Pre-review version of a scholarly article; closeMatch because preprints share structure but are not peer-reviewed
Planned collaborative endeavor
Formal document presenting information for a specific audience
Organized system supplying functions required by end users
Computer program in source or compiled form
Resource primarily intended to be heard
A Standard establishes a reference model; DefinedTermSet is the vocabulary it defines; relatedMatch because a Standard is a document not a term set
Time-stamped research plan shared before study execution; MedicalStudy is the closest schema.org class though too narrow for non-medical fields
Generic textual resource not covered by a more specific type
Structured sequence of steps for reproducible execution; closeMatch because HowTo covers instructional content rather than executable pipelines
Fallback to the most general schema.org creative work class
Additional persistent identifier for the same resource; modelled as an extra schema:PropertyValue alongside the DOI
Scheme or namespace of the alternate identifier (e.g. URL)
Related identifier for another resource; citation is the most common use; specific relation is qualified by relationType (see vocab rows below)
Scheme of the related identifier (e.g. DOI)
The nature of the relation; individual vocab values drive specific schema.org properties (see below)
Metadata scheme of the related resource (optional)
URL of the related identifier scheme
Encoding format or protocol of the related identifier scheme
Inverse of Cites; schema:citation has no explicit inverse property
This resource cites another; maps directly to schema:citation
Supplement relationship; no dedicated schema.org property
Inverse supplement relationship
Continuation relationship; no dedicated schema.org property
Continues a prior resource; no dedicated schema.org property
A separate resource describes this one
This resource describes another
A metadata document exists for this resource
This resource is metadata for another
New version derived from a prior version; isBasedOn is the closest schema.org term
Inverse of IsNewVersionOf
This resource is part of another
This resource has a part
Published within a journal or collection; isPartOf is the closest schema.org term
Inverse of References; schema:citation has no explicit inverse
This resource references another; citation is the closest but implies scholarly citation
Documentation exists for this resource
This resource documents another
Compiled/built from another resource
This resource compiles others
Variant form derived from an original
Original from which a variant was derived
Same content as another resource; schema:sameAs is the closest schema.org property
A review of this resource exists
This resource is a review of another
This resource is derived from another source
Inverse of IsDerivedFrom; schema:isBasedOn has no explicit inverse
Another resource depends on this one
This resource depends on another
This resource supersedes another; no dedicated schema.org property
This resource has been superseded
This resource is collected by another (e.g. a harvester)
This resource collects others
Direct derivation from a prior work
Inverse of IsBasedOn; schema:isBasedOn has no explicit inverse
Version relationship to a more general resource; isBasedOn is the closest schema.org term
Inverse of IsVersionOf
This resource is a translation of another; new in DataCite 4.6
This resource has a translated version; new in DataCite 4.6
File size or extent of the resource
Media or file format (e.g. application/pdf)
Version string or number of the resource
Rights statement or license; closeMatch because DataCite rights covers any rights statement while schema:license is specific to licensing
Resolvable URL of the license or rights statement
Machine-readable license code (e.g. CC-BY-4.0); modelled as the value of a PropertyValue
Scheme of the rights identifier (e.g. SPDX)
Resolvable URL of the rights identifier scheme
Free-text description of the resource
An abstract maps directly to the dedicated schema:abstract property on CreativeWork
Methodological description; no dedicated schema.org slot; use schema:description with a label
Series context information; no dedicated schema.org slot
Table of contents text; no dedicated schema.org slot
Technical details; no dedicated schema.org slot
Any other description type; fallback to schema:description
Spatial region where data was gathered or about which data is focused
Named place; serialised as a schema:Place with schema:name
A single lat/long point; serialised as a schema:GeoCoordinates object
Latitude of the point
Longitude of the point
Bounding box; serialised as a schema:GeoShape using the schema:box property (space-separated lat/long string)
Western boundary; one component of the GeoShape.box string; relatedMatch because four bounds combine into one string
Eastern boundary; one component of the GeoShape.box string
Southern boundary; one component of the GeoShape.box string
Northern boundary; one component of the GeoShape.box string
Polygon shape; serialised as a schema:GeoShape using the schema:polygon property; closeMatch because polygon encoding differs
Funding information; schema:funding links a CreativeWork to a schema:Grant
Name of the funding organization (value of funder Organization's schema:name)
Persistent identifier for the funder (e.g. ROR); modelled as a PropertyValue
Type of the funder identifier (e.g. ROR)
Resolvable URL of the funder identifier scheme
Grant or award number; modelled as the identifier of the schema:Grant
Resolvable URL of the grant or award
Title of the grant or award (value of schema:Grant's schema:name)
Structured bibliographic metadata for a related item; citation is the most common use; relationType qualifies the specific relationship
Type of the related item (e.g. JournalArticle); same controlled list as resourceTypeGeneral
Nature of the relation; see relationType vocab rows for specific schema.org property mappings
Identifier of the related item; modelled as a PropertyValue
Scheme of the related item identifier (e.g. DOI)
Metadata scheme used for the related item
Resolvable URL of the related item metadata scheme
Encoding format or protocol of the related item scheme
Creator of the related item
Name of the related item's creator
Title of the related item
Type qualifier for the related item title; see titleType vocab rows
Publication year of the related item
Journal or series volume of the related item
Journal issue number of the related item
Report or article number within a series
Type of the number (Article/Chapter/Report/Other)
Article number within an issue
Chapter position within a book
Report number within a series
Fallback for other number types
First page of the related item
Last page of the related item
Publisher of the related item
Edition of the related book or chapter
Contributing agent for the related item
Role of the related item's contributor
Name of the related item's contributor
The top-level DataCite resource; resourceTypeGeneral drives the specific schema.org subtype (e.g. schema:Dataset)
A DataCite Identifier is serialised as a schema:PropertyValue with propertyID=DOI and value=<doi-string>
The DataCite Title class is structurally a labelled text value + language tag; schema:Text (a schema.org DataType) is the closest class-level match
DataCite Publisher is always an Organization in schema.org
A year value; closeMatch because schema:Date represents a full ISO 8601 date while publicationYear is year-only
When subject carries a valueURI and subjectScheme it is serialised as a schema:DefinedTerm within a schema:DefinedTermSet; free-text subjects have no class equivalent
Contributor is a Person when nameType=Personal
Contributor is an Organization when nameType=Organizational
A Date+dateType block is modelled as a schema:PropertyValue (propertyID=dateType, value=ISO-8601 date string)
schema:Language exists and directly corresponds to a language designation
ResourceType drives the @type selection on the CreativeWork; no ResourceType class in schema.org
Same serialisation as Identifier; an additional PropertyValue alongside the primary DOI
A RelatedIdentifier points to another CreativeWork; the identifier string becomes that resource's schema:identifier
Size with optional unit (e.g. bytes, pages); schema:QuantitativeValue carries value+unitCode; closeMatch because DataCite Size is a free-text string
No Format class in schema.org; the format string becomes the schema:encodingFormat property on a MediaObject or CreativeWork
No Version class in schema.org; the version string becomes the schema:version property on the CreativeWork
No Rights class in schema.org; rights information becomes the schema:license property on the CreativeWork
A Description+descriptionType block is modelled as a schema:PropertyValue (propertyID=descriptionType, value=text); descriptionType=Abstract maps directly to schema:abstract
A spatial region or named place; schema:Place with schema:geo (GeoCoordinates or GeoShape) is the closest container class
A FundingReference contains funder + award information exactly matching the schema:Grant structure
A related item with full bibliographic metadata is a schema:CreativeWork; relatedItemType drives the specific subtype
Person fielding inquiries about the resource; schema:contactPoint (linking to ContactPoint) is the closest property
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="DataCollector" on a Role wrapper
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="DataCurator"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="DataManager"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="Distributor"
Editorial role maps directly to schema:editor on CreativeWork
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="HostingInstitution"
Fallback contributor role; expressed as roleName="Other"
Producer role maps directly to schema:producer on CreativeWork
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="ProjectLeader"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="ProjectManager"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="ProjectMember"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="RegistrationAgency"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="RegistrationAuthority"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="RelatedPerson"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="ResearchGroup"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="Researcher"
Rights holder maps directly to schema:copyrightHolder on CreativeWork
Sponsor maps directly to schema:sponsor on CreativeWork/Event
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="Supervisor"
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="Translator"; see also schema:translationOfWork for the work relationship
No dedicated schema.org property; expressed as roleName="WorkPackageLeader"
Each vertex of the polygon is a lat/long pair; serialised as a schema:GeoCoordinates object
DataCite-specific interior/containment point for the polygon; closest schema.org type is GeoCoordinates; closeMatch because inPolygonPoint has no direct schema.org equivalent
